eyebrow: C4 Dynamic · runtime flow — Search (index + query)
title: Execution order: how text-search uses Elasticsearch
subtitle: What ES is for: full-text search over users / organizations / workspaces. Two independent paths — ①–③ INDEX (keep ES in sync with Mongo) · ④–⑦ QUERY (serve a search).

steps.detail: ① INDEX — an entity (user / org / workspace) is created or changed in the source-of-truth Mongo.
① INDEX — worker pushes the changed document to text-search.
① INDEX — Elasticsearch Client writes/updates the doc in the search index. ES is now in sync.
② QUERY — user types a search ("find a user / workspace").
② QUERY — api forwards the query string + scope.
② QUERY — Search Handler runs the full-text query against the index.
② QUERY — ranked results returned to the user.

rels: MongoDB is great at exact lookups by id, not full-text / ranked search across many fields
Elasticsearch holds a derived, rebuildable index — Mongo `main` stays the source of truth
If ES is lost, re-index from Mongo (no data loss)
refs: static structure: text-search (L3, Part S) — callers: api-admin / api-auth / api-workspace / worker-* — zoom out: Container view Supporting services (L2, §7)
caption: Identity Platform · C4 Dynamic · Search index + query — how text-search keeps and serves the Elasticsearch index
